C[Haikunature: Hi there! I accidentally accepted some other random game so it took me a while to get to your game.
Haikunature: I'm about to have dinner so I may be out for a couple of hours

C[Haikunature: Sorry I left the Wed night Go group without saying goodbye to you! I was busy in my head telling myself that it wasn't too embarrassing that I lost two games to someone who said he was a beginner and the last time he had played was ten years ago. Well, that's what he said...
C[squidlarkin: well, you might make up for it with this game
C[Haikunature: a six handicap game and you still have me in gote!
C[squidlarkin: sente's all I've got! well, and two and a half corners
C[Haikunature: oh that wasstupid of me!
squidlarkin: you wanna take it back?
Haikunature: Only if this were a teaching game for me. So let's keep playing but maybe the next time we play we will make it a teaching game.
C[squidlarkin: not a teaching game, but this will still be a lesson in containment
Haikunature: Oh that's a good idea
C[Haikunature: I agree! I am admiring your moves.
C[Haikunature: just ot a phone call back in 20 min!
C[Haikunature: I don't think Go is good for one's heart!!

C[Haikunature: By the way, clearly you have won so anytime you want to call it a game just let me know. I am learning a lot playing this to the bitter end though, so it's really up to whenever you are getting tired of this game.
C[squidlarkin: I'm curious about the score, and it never hurts to have more yose practice
C[squidlarkin: it's safe to hane here fyi
C[Haikunature: Yeah I realized you were just eating up my territory but I thought if I got to the 14 line you couldn't hane me
Haikunature: for whatever that might be worth
squidlarkin: black A11 would have forced me to connect, giving you the chance to protect the cutting point
squidlarkin: you were right to not block at A10 though

C[Haikunature: I did always wonder how one decides the game is over in the sense that there are no more points to be won or lost. usually it is apparent who has won and one person just concedes. so this is interesting for me.
squidlarkin: at the higher levels, it's apparently really common for games to come down to a few points
C[Haikunature: I was trying to see if I could somehow reduce your score by having th 19 line filled but I think I would just lose all my stones in that area.

Haikunature: because there was never any way for me to make eyes? I am always curious about when tesujis come in handy. it seems like the opportunity to use a tesuji are very rare and yet it is a big part of Go study, isn't it?
C[squidlarkin: opportunities for the eye-stealing tesuji are plentiful. I did it twice in this game, even
C[Haikunature: the score estimator has you winning by 118.5! That's a record I think
squidlarkin: oh I didn't know you could use that during a game!
C[Haikunature: Is it considered in bad form to do that? I find it entertaining. It isn't accurate at the beginning but by the end I think it is accurate.
squidlarkin: I just know I've resigned a bunch of games that it then told me I was winning
Haikunature: One can also use the analyze thing but I try not to do that because it actually doesn't help me get better or trust my playing
Haikunature: Also maybe you could show me the places where you used a tesuji (if you recall)
squidlarkin: if we wanted to end it properly there are 2 or 3 moves that still need to be made
squidlarkin: and then we'd pass, yes
Haikunature: Oh how does one do a review?
squidlarkin: I haven't really used it much, did you click the link?
Haikunature: no i thought you did
squidlarkin: I made the link so you can click it
